add.attestBody: I attest that I am permitted to ingest this source (its ToS or the author's license allows personal copying). Shelf will still obey robots.txt and rate limits.
add.crawlIndexHint: Crawl this whole site into the discovery index (every title it lists)
add.crawlIndexInfo: (admin) crawls a whole site to discover every title — the results appear on the Catalog page. Needs a full site URL.
add.crawlIndexNeedsUrl: Index needs a full site URL
add.crawlScheduleBody: Throttle how fast / how much this title's background crawl runs, and restrict it to certain hours. Leave blank to use the source defaults. (Editable later in the Sources page.)
add.crawlScheduleSubtitle: Optional — defaults are fine for most sites
add.grabTitleHint: Add this one title to your library and start fetching it
add.grabTitleInfo: adds a single title from the selected source and backfills its chapters into your library.
add.indexingStarted: Indexing started — watch progress on Jobs
add.intro: Shelf only ingests sources you are permitted to read. Choose a source, then hook a title.
add.legallyOwn: Only import files you legally own.
add.mapFolderDesc: Shelf imports every EPUB / TXT / Markdown / PDF / CBZ / CBR file in this directory and watches it — new and changed files appear in your library automatically.
add.notEnabledPre: This source is not enabled. Enable it (and confirm you're permitted) on the

add.titleAdded: Title added — backfilling into your library
add.unmapFolderMessage: Stop watching “{{path}}”? Imported works from this folder are removed from your library (the files on disk are untouched).
add.updateIndexed: Update already-indexed content (re-fetch pages crawled before). Off by default: re-adding a source resumes without repeating what was already indexed.
add.uploadFormats: Upload EPUB / TXT / Markdown / PDF / CBZ / CBR
add.workReferencePlaceholder: Paste a link, ID or slug…

panel.desc: Group titles into named shelves and tune each shelf's automation.
panel.empty: No shelves yet — group titles into one with “+ New shelf”.
panel.hint: Bookshelves group your library however you like. Each shelf can auto-send new chapters to Kindle, email or notify you when a title is added, act as a Goodreads destination, and (for admins) watch a folder for new books. Manage which titles are on a shelf from the 🗂 Shelves button on any work, or filter the library by shelf in Browse.
panel.title: Bookshelves

settings.deleteConfirm: Delete shelf “{{name}}”? The titles stay in your library.
settings.downloadTitle: Download every work on this shelf as EPUBs (ZIP)
settings.watchPath: Monitored path (admin) — new books found here are added to this shelf and trigger its notify / Kindle / email actions

comic.noImages: No images in this chapter yet — the crawler may still be fetching the pages.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare Access
cloudflare.hint: Automatically add a new user's email to a Cloudflare Zero Trust Access application policy when you create or approve them (and remove it when you delete them). The API token needs “Access: Apps and Policies: Edit”.

audiobooks.subtitle: {{count}} in the shared audio library
audiobooks.emptyHint: Audiobooks appear here once they've been acquired into the shared pool.

discover.emptyHint: They appear as the crawler enriches discovered works with genres.
discover.fulltextPrompt: Type to search the full text of indexed pages.
discover.modeFulltextHint: Search inside the full text of every indexed page
discover.noMatchesHint: Nothing in the indexed page text matches “{{query}}”.
discover.noMatchingTitlesHint: No discovered titles match your search or filters.
discover.searchingFulltext: Searching the full text of indexed pages

featured.infoHint: The rules for the big billboard title at the top of the Discover page: how it's picked, which genres + media types it's drawn from, and how often it changes. Applied on top of each user's permission-filtered catalog, so it can only ever narrow what they're already allowed to see.
featured.methodPopularHint: Drawn from the top of the popularity ranking.
featured.rotationHint: 0 = a lively carousel that changes each visit. Any other value pins one title for everyone for that many hours (168 = a steady “featured this week”).
featured.title: Featured this week

box.noLookupsYet: — no lookups yet; run Test to verify
box.perUserHint: Per-user — connect your own shelf under Settings → Goodreads.

cards.acquisitionBlurb: Library managers (Readarr / Kapowarr) fill the catalog; the usenet pipeline (Prowlarr → SABnzbd) and the torrent pipeline (Prowlarr → qBittorrent) fetch books on demand. VirusTotal scans torrent-grabbed files before import; the Cloudflare solver lets protected sources be read. Connect a service, test it, tune its limits.
cards.metadataBlurb: The sources of truth for author, synopsis, cover, chapter / volume counts, and matching. Connect the ones that fit your library; each box explains what it provides and how it's matched. Defaults keep request rates polite — tune them per provider if you hit limits.
cards.readingAppsBlurb: Connect Audiobookshelf and Storyteller. Shelf makes your stocked ebooks + audiobooks available to them (Audiobookshelf auto-scans the shared folders; Storyteller gets EPUB + audio copies, converted on demand). Any title they have in only one format — ebook without its audiobook, or vice-versa — is fetched by Shelf and delivered back.

cf.desc: Passes Cloudflare / Turnstile challenges via a FlareSolverr proxy.
cf.name: Cloudflare solver
cf.urlHint: blank = disabled (falls back to the in-app browser)
cf.useBody: A FlareSolverr-compatible proxy that solves Cloudflare / Turnstile challenges so protected sources can be read. Blank URL disables it (falls back to the in-app browser). Changes are honored immediately — no restart.

field.absKeyHint: API key from Audiobookshelf → Settings → Users → API Keys. Point an ABS library at Shelf's stock / audiobook folders so it auto-scans them.
field.annasKeyPlaceholderNew: paste your Anna's Archive secret key
field.dailyCapTitle: Global fast-downloads per UTC day — Anna's membership quota. The worker parks the queue once spent.
field.downloadDirHint: blank = use the SABnzbd library path
field.downloadDirPlaceholder: Download dir (blank = use the SABnzbd library path)
field.fetchWantedAbs: Fetch the missing format of single-format items (wanted)
field.fetchWantedStory: Fetch the missing half of read-alongs that have only one format (wanted)
field.formatsPlaceholder: Preferred formats, best first (epub, azw3, mobi, pdf)
field.goodreadsUserPlaceholder: Goodreads numeric user ID (or profile URL)
field.importPathPlaceholder: Import path — a shared folder Storyteller reads (e.g. /mnt/NAS-Pool/media/Storyteller)
field.indexerIdsPlaceholder: Restrict to indexer IDs (comma separated — blank = all)
field.keepAfterImport: Keep the torrent after import (seed manually; default deletes it)
field.qbDownloadPathPlaceholder: qBittorrent download path (its own view, e.g. /media/NAS-Pool/media/Downloads/shelf)
field.remotePathMappingQb: Remote path mapping (qBittorrent path → Shelf path; only if on another host)
field.remotePathMappingSab: Remote path mapping (only if SABnzbd is on another host)
field.vtBlockUnknown: Hold files VirusTotal has never seen (default: allow unknown)

hint.absKey: API key from Audiobookshelf → Settings → Users → API Keys. Point an ABS library at Shelf's stock / audiobook folders so it auto-scans them.
hint.annasKey: The apijson secret key from your Anna's Archive membership (FAQ → API). It enables the fast-download API — the only route that reliably bypasses the overloaded free mirrors. Stored server-side and never returned. Free MD5 downloads via the LibGen mirrors still work without it.
hint.importPath: Shelf copies EPUB + audiobook into the import path (converting to EPUB on demand) and triggers alignment. Copies — Storyteller never edits your originals.
hint.qbDownloadPath: Where qBittorrent saves grabs — its OWN path. It must land on storage Shelf can also read; if qBittorrent is on another host with a different mount point, set the mapping below so Shelf can find the files.
hint.vtKey: Every torrent-grabbed file is SHA-256 hashed and checked against VirusTotal before import; flagged files are deleted and an alert is raised. Files are never uploaded (lookup only).

jobs.blankHint: Blank = source default. Hours are UTC.
jobs.deleteConfirmMessage: Stop and delete the crawl job for “{{title}}”? It won't auto-restart; gathered chapters are kept.
jobs.deleteTitle: Delete this job and stop the crawl (won't auto-restart). Gathered chapters are kept; resume later with Renew or the work's 'Check for updates'.
jobs.renewTitle: Re-queue failed chapters and run this job again

layout.infoHint: The default arrangement (category + genre order, and what's hidden) every user starts from. Each user can still override it for themselves via “Edit layout” on the Index page. This is applied ON TOP of each user's permission-filtered catalog, so reordering or hiding here can never expose a category or 18+ genre a user isn't allowed to see — restricted content is simply absent for them.
layout.noCategories: No discovered categories yet — index a site first.
layout.reorderHint: Reorder with ▲▼ and Hide/Show each category & genre, then save it as the default for everyone.
layout.title: Global default index layout

check.noNewChaptersSource: This title's source doesn't get new chapters.

libgrid.hookedFromTitle: Hooked from chapter {{chapter}} — earlier chapters were skipped
libgrid.paused: Paused — automatic updates are off for this title.
libgrid.resumed: Resumed — checking for new chapters and gathering any outstanding ones.

series.fetchingMissing_other: Fetching {{count}} missing volumes — see the Sources page

series.missingWarning_other: {{count}} books in this series are missing from your library.

series.removeConfirmMessage_other: Remove all {{count}} owned volumes of “{{name}}” from your library? (Volumes you don't own are unaffected.)

home.heroEmptyHint: Browse the index to find and hook a title, or import a file you own.

statusHelp.complete: The series has finished and every chapter is gathered.
statusHelp.incomplete: Some chapters are missing or couldn't be fetched.
statusHelp.ongoing: Caught up — new chapters are gathered as the series releases them.
statusHelp.paused: Automatic updates are off — Resume to gather new chapters again.

add.alsoFetchSeries: Also fetch the rest of each title's series
add.catalogueHint: Each title is resolved and made browsable in Discovery — nothing is downloaded. You can fetch any of them later from its page.
add.downloadHint: Each title's file is fetched into your library as it's resolved.
add.emptyListHint: Nothing was returned for this list.
add.followSeries: Follow each series for new volumes
add.largeListHint: Too many to pick through by hand, so Shelf {{verb}} the whole list in the background — resolving each title gradually (watch the progress bar on this page). Already-resolved titles are remembered, so it never redoes work, and you can pause it any time.
add.sendToStock: Send to operator stock instead of a library
add.sendToStockHint: Shared pre-fetch pool, not added to any user's library.
add.seriesHint: Applies per fetched title that's part of a series. The first fills in earlier and later volumes now; following keeps future volumes coming even after they leave the list.
add.title: Import a reading list
add.toStockHint: New titles go to shared operator stock — no library or bookshelf.
add.uncheckedHint: Unchecked titles are remembered but never fetched. Checked titles start {{verb}} now.

edit.alsoFetchSeries: Also fetch the rest of each title's series
edit.followSeries: Follow each series for new volumes
edit.sendToStockHint: Shared pre-fetch pool, not a library.
edit.seriesHint: Applies per fetched title that's part of a series. Following keeps future volumes coming even after they leave the list.
edit.title: Import settings

manage.emptyHint: Import a list to pull in its titles and keep it in sync as you add more.
manage.intro: Import a reading list or library from AniList, Goodreads, Open Library, Hardcover, MyAnimeList, or an Amazon wishlist. Shelf keeps watching it — new titles you add there are fetched here automatically.
manage.title: List imports

manageRow.catalogueTitle: Titles are resolved into Discovery, not downloaded
manageRow.followingTitle: Follows each series for new volumes
manageRow.removeConfirmMessage: Stop monitoring “{{name}}”? Already-fetched titles stay; no new ones will be added.

manageRow.toStockTitle: New titles go to shared operator stock

refHint.goodreads: your Goodreads numeric user-id or profile URL

strip.noTitles: No titles on this list right now.

admin.desc: Operator alerts, the global fallback channel, and broadcasts.
admin.hint: Operator alerts (health, errors, failed jobs, integration & backup status) go to every admin's channels for the events enabled here. The global channel is a fallback target for admins who haven't set up their own. The broadcast notifies all users.
admin.title: Admin notifications

channels.hintPre: Connect where your notifications go — a phone push app (ntfy, Pushover), Telegram, a Discord/Slack webhook, or email. Add as many as you like. Choose
channels.title: Notification channels

kind.emailHelp: Emails the personal address set under Delivery, via the shared mail server.
kind.ntfyHelp: Install the ntfy app, pick a topic name, and subscribe to it.
kind.telegramHelp: Create a bot with @BotFather, message it, then enter your chat id.

prefs.desc: Pick which events reach your channels.
prefs.title: Notify me about…

controls.comicHint: Pinch or double-tap a page to zoom; arrows / taps turn pages.
controls.readingModeHint: Auto picks Scroll for webtoons/manhua and Pages for manga, by what you’re reading.
controls.textCleanupHint: Fix badly-scraped chapters — remove censoring dots and reflow the wall of text into readable paragraphs.
controls.workModeHint: Disguise the reader to look like work content.

reader.loadErrorBody: Something went wrong fetching this work.
reader.notFetched: This chapter hasn’t been fetched yet — the slow crawler may still be working.

related.confirmMatchTitle: Confirm this is the right match (locks it from re-scoring)
related.providerMoreTitle: Provider lists {{count}} more chapters than we've gathered
related.weMoreTitle: We have {{count}} more chapters than the provider lists

send.kindleHint: For Kindle, add the sender address to your Amazon “Approved Personal Document E-mail List” first.
send.notConfiguredPost: — meanwhile you can download the EPUB and use Amazon’s Send-to-Kindle app or USB.
send.notConfiguredPre: Email delivery isn’t configured. Add your SMTP login in
send.title: Send / export

account.changePasswordDesc: Enter your current password to set a new one.
account.desc: Your login username, display name, and email.
account.title: Account
account.usernameHint: Your login name — used to sign in (must be unique).

adult.disabled: Explicit 18+ content is disabled on this instance by an administrator.
adult.hint: Show explicit 18+ content in these categories. On by default — turn off any category you don't want to see. Only categories an administrator permits are shown here, and your choice applies to your account only.
adult.title: Adult content (18+)

performance.title: Performance
performance.hint: Turn off animated background, blur and other GPU-heavy effects on this device. Auto-enabled on laptops / low-power devices; set it here to override. Saved for this device only.

appearance.heading: Appearance
appearance.themeDesc: Pick a theme — every surface, the reader, and covers retint instantly.

autoBackup.desc: Scheduled instance backups so an unattended install isn't left with zero backups.
autoBackup.enableSubtext: Pruned beyond the kept count; uploads are never pruned.
autoBackup.hint: Scheduled instance backups so an unattended install isn't left with zero backups. App-created backups beyond the kept count are pruned (uploads are never pruned).
autoBackup.title: Automatic backups

backup.desc: Snapshots a fresh or existing Shelf install can restore from.
backup.hint: Snapshots a fresh (or existing) Shelf install can restore from. Backups created here and ones you upload from another machine both appear below as selectable objects — pick one to restore, choosing per section what to bring in.
backup.instanceEmpty: This instance is empty — everything in the backup is selected.
backup.instanceHasData: This instance already has data — pick what to bring in. Skipped sections are left untouched.
backup.legendAtomic: The whole restore is atomic — if anything fails it rolls back and nothing changes.
backup.legendMerge: adds new items and keeps existing ones ·
backup.legendReplace: erases that section here first, then loads the backup's (can't be undone).
backup.levelDataDetail: Everything in the database, incl. chapter text, the discovery catalog and crawl index. No re-crawl/re-index — only images (comic pages, covers) re-fetch.
backup.levelFullDetail: The full database plus every media file (comic pages, cached covers). A complete clone — nothing is re-gathered. Can be very large.
backup.levelSettingsDetail: Config, library, progress + crawl position. The catalog re-indexes and chapter content re-downloads on the new install.
backup.modeMergeHint: Add items from the backup; keep what's already here.
backup.modeReplaceHint: Delete this section here, then load the backup's.
backup.modeSkipHint: Leave this instance's data as-is — don't import.
backup.newerVersionTip: This backup was made by a newer Shelf version — upgrade before restoring
backup.noBackups: No backups yet. Create one above, or upload an existing .zip.
backup.restoredToast: Restored {{count}} records from the {{level}} backup{{warnings}}. Reloading…
backup.snapDeleteMsg: Delete the database snapshot “{{name}}”? This frees disk but the snapshot can't be recovered.
backup.snapExplainPost: data with that exact snapshot and restarts the server — the current database is safety-copied first.
backup.snapExplainPre: Whole-database file copies kept next to the live database (automatic pre-operation safety copies and recovery files). Restoring one replaces
backup.snapRestartMsg: Restoring — the server is restarting. This page will reconnect in a moment.
backup.snapRestoreMsg: Replace ALL current data with “{{name}}” and restart the server? The current database is safety-copied first, but everything since this snapshot is lost and any in-progress work is interrupted.
backup.title: Backups

blocklist.hint: URLs and domains you've removed from the index. They won't be re-discovered by crawls or hooked. Unblock to allow them again.
blocklist.title: Blocked content

bookCatalog.closeness: Closeness threshold (0–1; lower = more API calls)
bookCatalog.hint: A hybrid book catalog: a persistent hot set of popular titles (seeded from Open Library trending + popular subjects and Google Books) plus live resolve — a search with no close local match is looked up against the book APIs on the fly and cached. Add a Google Books integration with an API key to lift its keyless quota; Open Library needs no key.
bookCatalog.title: Book catalog

crawl.hint: How fast the backfill + index crawlers run. Changes apply live to running and future jobs — no restart. Each source's own rate limits (set per-source on Sources) still apply, so raising these never bypasses per-site politeness. Lower interval + higher chapters/parallel = faster but more load on sources (and a higher chance of rate-limiting). Backfill and indexing have independent budgets, so they no longer slow each other down when run together.
crawl.title: Crawl speed

crawlIdentity.envNote: Keep this honest and reachable — it's how sites identify your self-hosted crawler and how a site owner contacts you. (Env defaults:
crawlIdentity.hint: How the polite fetcher identifies itself to every source: a User-Agent (project name + contact link, matched against each site's robots.txt) and a contact email (sent as the From header so a site admin can reach you). Changes apply live to running and future crawls — no restart. Leave a field blank to reset it to the built-in default.
crawlIdentity.title: Crawl identity

discovery.layoutSubtitle: Reorder or hide categories and lanes for everyone who hasn't customised their own.

contentLanguages.title: Content languages
contentLanguages.hint: Which languages Shelf grabs and stocks. The crawler and metadata providers are queried for these, and titles are tagged with a language badge. Applies instance-wide.

fetchPriority.hint: The instance-wide order Shelf tries acquisition sources in — it uses the first that can deliver a title. Applies to every user and every auto-fetch. Move the most-preferred to the top. (Admin-only, global.)
fetchPriority.title: Fetch source priority

settings.footer: Shelf ingests only sources you are permitted to read. See the README for the full sourcing policy.

indexing.advancedSubtitle: Crawl-default caps and the comix browser crawler
indexing.footPre: Crawls also obey robots.txt and each source's rate limits (set per-source on
indexing.hint: New index crawls run with no page cap — they keep indexing until the whole site is covered. After a long stretch with nothing new (no title, no new link) they stop looking for more pages but still finish whatever's queued, so nothing found is left behind. This is the default threshold for new crawls; override it per-crawl on the Jobs page.
indexing.idleLabel: Stop discovering after this many pages with nothing new (the crawl still finishes its queue)
indexing.title: Indexing

insights.telemetrySubtitle: Raw request-stats, VirusTotal usage and pipeline tables

kindle.hint: Set where your EPUBs go — your Kindle and/or your own inbox — then use the 📤 Send button on any work. Mail is sent from the shared address configured by an administrator; for Kindle, add that address to your Amazon "Approved Personal Document E-mail List". If the mail server hasn't been configured yet, sending is off.
kindle.personalHint: Your account email — “Send to email” delivers here.
kindle.title: Send to Kindle / email

language.desc: The language for Shelf’s interface. Book content is unchanged.
language.label: Interface language
language.title: Language

listening.autoplayNextHint: Continue to the next track automatically when one finishes.
listening.desc: Defaults for the audiobook player — applied to every book you play.
listening.playbackSpeedHint: Used when a book opens. Changing speed in the player updates this too.
listening.title: Listening

missingRecheck.autoRequestSeries: Auto-request the rest of a series when you fetch one of its books
missingRecheck.everyDays: Re-check unavailable titles every N days
missingRecheck.hint: Titles Shelf couldn't find are parked and periodically re-attempted. These control how often a parked title becomes due again and how many are re-checked each run.
missingRecheck.title: Re-checking unavailable titles

smtp.hint: The shared mail server every user sends through (Send-to-Kindle, shelf auto-Kindle, notifications). Users only set their own destination address — they never see these credentials. Add the From address to each Kindle's Approved Personal Document list.
smtp.title: Email server (SMTP)

settings.title: Settings

shelfPrompt.inStockHint: In-stock formats are added instantly; anything not in stock is queued to fetch.
shelfPrompt.saveToStockHint: Pre-fetched into the shared pool — every user can then add it to their library instantly.

sources.deleteConfirmBody: Permanently delete this source and all its indexed pages, catalog entries and search records? This can't be undone. (To bring it back instead, use Restore.)
sources.hookAllHint: Add every fetched page to your library as one work
sources.idleThresholdHint: After this many consecutive pages with nothing new (no title and no new link), stop discovering more pages — the crawl still finishes whatever is already queued

manage.enableConfirmMessage: Enable “{{name}}”? Only do this for sources you are permitted to read.
manage.headlessConfirmMessage: Slower and heavier — use for JS-heavy sites you are permitted to read.
manage.minIntervalHint: Gathering is paced only by this interval — there is no daily cap.
manage.robotsConfirmMessage: Only for dev/troubleshooting on sources you are permitted to read.
manage.tabHint: Each source carries a compliance declaration. The engine refuses to ingest any source that is not explicitly permitted — toggle a source on only for content you have the right to read.
manage.tokenHint: Stored on the server and never returned by the API. Used only to fetch content your account is entitled to. For J-Novel, this is your account access token.

sources.noIndexedSourcesHint: Add a crawl source to discover titles.
sources.nothingFetchingHint: Acquire a title or hook a work to start a job.
sources.removeConfirmPost: — re-add the URL (or hit Restore) later to resume without re-crawling. Or delete everything permanently.
sources.removeConfirmPre: Remove this source? Crawling stops, but the indexed pages, catalog entries and search records are
sources.restoreHint: Resume crawling from where it left off — already-indexed pages aren't re-fetched
sources.subtitle: Live operator view — pause, resume or inspect any job.
sources.timeSpentHint: Total crawl time, summed across all sites (parallel crawls each count)
sources.title: Sources & Acquisitions
sources.titleHint: Crawl jobs, downloads, indexed sources and watched folders — everything Shelf is fetching for you.
sources.usedForHint: Limit this source to the chosen media. It's then skipped when searching for other media types — e.g. a comics-only source won't be queried for novel requests.

pipeline.infoHint: How acquisition went: where downloads succeeded (usenet / torrent / Anna's Archive / LibriVox), web-crawl hooks, and — for titles that couldn't be obtained — why.
pipeline.title: Pipeline fetches

req.chartCategoryAria: Requests per hour over time, by category
req.chartOutcomeAria: Requests per hour over time, by outcome
req.infoCategory: Requests per hour (Y) over clock time (X), stacked by request category — crawl, metadata, integration, image, libgen, solver, export, etc.
req.infoHint: Every outbound HTTP request the app makes — crawling, metadata APIs, integrations (Prowlarr/SABnzbd), ingestion, cover/image fetches and Cloudflare solvers — counted by destination host and category. Updated every ~30s.
req.infoOutcome: Requests per hour (Y) over clock time (X). One line per outcome — success, blocked (anti-bot / rate-limit), timeout, error — plus a dashed Total.
req.noPerCategory: No per-category data for this window yet.
req.title: External requests
req.topDestinationsHint: The external hosts the app talked to most in this window.

vt.connectHint: Connect VirusTotal under Integrations to see usage.
vt.infoHint: Torrent-grabbed files are SHA-256 hashed and checked against VirusTotal before import. Counts over the last 30 days.
vt.noLookups: No lookups yet — run a Test on the VirusTotal integration to verify it works.
vt.title: VirusTotal API usage

caps.intro: Throttle how much the stocker does per day across all batches.
caps.title: Daily stocking caps

feeding.desc: List subscriptions set to stock — new titles are auto-stocked as they appear. Managed under List imports below.
feeding.title: Lists feeding stock

header.intro: Pre-download catalog works through the pipeline and keep them on disk, so when a user acquires one it's served instantly. Needs the pipeline configured under
header.notEnabled: The Prowlarr + SABnzbd pipeline isn't fully enabled yet — stocking can't run until it is.
header.stockDir: Stock directory (kept apart from user downloads)

jobsList.emptyHint: Queue a selection above — give it a name to track it here.

modal.deleteConfirm: Delete the “{{name}}” batch ({{count}} item(s))? The stocked files stay on disk so already-served titles keep working.
modal.subtitle: {{stocked}}/{{total}} stocked · {{inFlight}} in progress

panel.queueBatchSub: Pick what to stock — or stock the whole catalog
panel.title: Stocking

queue.entireCatalogHint: Ignore the filters; cap still applies
queue.name: Name
queue.notReady: Set a stock directory + pipeline first.
queue.noteNothing: Nothing new to stock — all {{skipped}} matched titles are already queued.
queue.noteQueued: Created “{{name}}” — {{queued}} title(s) queued (skipped {{skipped}} already stocked, {{selected}} matched).

download.audiobookHint: Where audiobooks are stored, separate from ebooks. Blank = a sibling “Audiobooks” directory next to the books library.
download.hint: Where the acquisition pipelines drop files before they're imported into the pool. SAB path mappings translate the SABnzbd host's paths to the paths this app reads.
download.libgenHint: Where the libgen/open-libraries pipeline saves downloads before import. Blank = the stock pool.
download.noSab: No SABnzbd integration — configure one under Integrations to set download paths.
download.sabLibraryHint: Where verified SAB downloads are promoted to. Blank = import in place from SAB's drop folder.
download.sabMappingsHint: Map the path SABnzbd reports (on its host) to the path this app can read (its mount). One row per mapping.
download.title: Download paths

migrate.hint: When a directory changes, also MOVE its current contents to the new path (skip-existing). Instant on the same filesystem; a slower recursive copy across mounts. Do this while the app is quiet. Without it, only NEW files use the new path.
migrate.label: Move existing files to the new locations on save

pool.backupsHint: Where app-created + uploaded backups are stored. Keep this OUTSIDE the image cache.
pool.coversHint: Durable cover thumbnails (never evicted; some exist only here). Kept on FAST LOCAL disk — small and read on every grid view — and auto-downscaled/compressed on save. Blank = local default.
pool.hint: The stock directory is the central on-disk pool where items live; a user's library is just a list of pointers into it (and into monitored folders, which act as secondary pools) — re-pointing a path only changes where NEW files are written/read, it does not move existing data. Blank a field to use the built-in default.
pool.mediaHint: The on-disk root for web-crawled/captured content: comic pages (comics/), book media (books/), descrambled captures, and the evictable image cache (imgcache/). All of this is a REBUILDABLE cache (re-extracted/re-fetched on demand) — best kept on a large NAS/network mount so it doesn't fill the local disk. Served at /media, so it needs a restart to remount.
pool.restartNote: Changing the image-cache path needs a restart to remount it; the others apply immediately.
pool.stockHint: Where stocked/pooled on-disk items are kept. The central pool every library points into.
pool.title: Library pool & directories

watched.empty: None. Add one from the Add page.
watched.hint: Folders watched for new titles. Each acts as a secondary on-disk pool: imported items stay where they are and libraries only point at them. Add/remove monitored folders on the Add page.
watched.subtitle: · secondary pools
watched.title: Monitored folders

cloudflare.clearanceHelp: How long a solved cf_clearance is replayed before re-solving (cf_clearance lasts ~30 min).
cloudflare.help: A FlareSolverr-compatible proxy used to pass Cloudflare challenges. Honored immediately.
cloudflare.solverUrlHelp: FlareSolverr-compatible endpoint. Blank disables it (falls back to the in-app browser). e.g. http://host:8191
cloudflare.timeoutHelp: Max seconds to let the solver work a single challenge before giving up.
cloudflare.title: Cloudflare solver

comix.chromePathHelp: Path to the Chromium binary for the solver/crawler. Blank = auto-detect the bundled Playwright build.
comix.enableHelp: Crawl comix.to via the headful browser. Needs zendriver + an X server (Xvfb) + Chromium. Off = skip comix.
comix.help: comix.to is read with an evasion-hardened browser (zendriver) that passes its Turnstile challenge.
comix.pagesPerTickHelp: How many /browse pages (28 titles each) to crawl per tick. Higher = faster catalog fill, more load.
comix.title: Comix crawler

crawl.frontierHelp: Safety ceiling on how far the pending queue may run ahead of fetching. Set well above any real catalog.
crawl.help: Defaults for NEW index crawls (per-site overrides live on the Jobs page).
crawl.idlePagesHelp: After this many consecutive pages with nothing new, stop discovering more (queued pages still drain).
crawl.maxDepthHelp: Loop-guard depth bound (URLs are de-duped). Keep loose so deep pagination is still reached.
crawl.maxPagesHelp: Hard page cap for a crawl. 0 = unlimited (stops on the idle threshold instead).
crawl.title: Crawl defaults

imgcache.capHelp: A periodic sweep LRU-evicts the image cache back under this. Cached images re-fetch on miss. 0 = no cap.
imgcache.help: On-disk LRU cache for covers + remote chapter images.
imgcache.title: Image cache

lists.help: How often Shelf re-polls imported reading lists (AniList, Goodreads, etc.) for newly-added titles.
lists.intervalHelp: Hours between automatic re-checks of every active list import. New titles found are fetched. Users can also 'Check now' on demand.
lists.title: List imports

logging.levelHelp: DEBUG shows per-tick crawl detail; INFO is the readable default.
logging.title: Logging

login.maxAttemptsHelp: Failed logins (per username + per IP) before a temporary lockout.
login.minPasswordHelp: Minimum characters required when setting a password.
login.title: Login & security
login.windowHelp: Sliding window / lockout duration for failed logins.

adultGate.desc: Which categories may surface explicit 18+ content.
adultGate.hint: Enabled per category by default; turn one off to hide its 18+ content for everyone, or leave all off to disable 18+ entirely. Each user can still narrow this further under their own settings.
adultGate.title: Adult content (18+)

defaultCategories.desc: Categories non-admins can view without a per-user override.
defaultCategories.hint: Applies to any non-admin user who has no per-user category cap. Admins always see every category.
defaultCategories.title: Default categories

defaultPermissions.desc: What non-admins can see and do without a per-user override.
defaultPermissions.hint: Managing sources, jobs, the crawler, integrations and backups always stays admin-only. Admins have everything.
defaultPermissions.title: Default permissions

defaultsSection.subtitle: Who can sign up, login security, and what new users inherit
defaultsSection.title: Registration & defaults

drawer.cantChangeOwnRole: You can't change your own role
drawer.cantDeleteSelf: You can't delete your own account
drawer.cantSignSelfOut: You can't sign yourself out here
drawer.sendInvite: Email login details to the user
drawer.sendInviteHint: Sends their username, this password, and how to sign in (requires SMTP configured under Notifications).
drawer.sendInviteNeedEmail: Add an email address above to enable this.
drawer.deleteConfirm: Delete user “{{name}}”? This can't be undone.
drawer.deleteProtectedHint: Deletion is protected. Enter the delete secret to confirm — or set the account inactive instead (reversible).
drawer.deleteProtectedLine: Hard-delete user “{{name}}” and all their data? This can't be undone.
drawer.disableConfirm: Disable “{{name}}”? They won't be able to sign in.
drawer.grantAdminConfirm: Make “{{name}}” an admin? They'll get full control of this instance.
drawer.inheritDefault: Inherit the default for normal users
drawer.logoutEverywhereConfirm: Sign “{{name}}” out of all devices? They'll need to sign in again.
drawer.usernameHint: The login name used to sign in (must be unique).

users.emptyHint: Add the first account with “+ Add user”.
users.noMatchHint: Try a different search or filter.

registration.approvalHint: — anyone can sign up, but an admin must approve them before they can sign in.
registration.desc: Who can create an account from the sign-in screen.
registration.openHint: — anyone can sign up and use the app immediately.
registration.title: Self-registration

users.subtitle: Everyone shares the same library; reading progress and settings are private to each account.
users.title: Users

wanted.emptyBreakdownHint: No user has requested or tracked anything on this instance.
wanted.emptyRequestsHint: Titles you request that Shelf can't find right away show up here while it keeps searching.
wanted.emptyTrackingHint: Follow a series or author from a catalog page to auto-fetch new releases.
wanted.pageHint: Titles you've requested and the series and authors you track. Requested titles keep being searched until they turn up; tracked series and authors auto-fetch new releases.
wanted.title: Wanted
wanted.trackPausedToast: Paused “{{name}}” — new releases won't be auto-fetched
wanted.trackRemoveMessage: Stop tracking “{{name}}”? New titles won't be auto-fetched.

chapters.emptyHint: Chapters appear here as they're gathered.

fixMeta.noMatches: No matches — or no metadata providers are enabled (Settings → Integrations). You can still edit the fields above by hand.
fixMeta.sourceRefPlaceholder: this title's ref / URL on the source — fix a wrong fetching source
fixMeta.title: Fix metadata

toast.following: Following — new titles will be gathered automatically.
toast.noNewChaptersSource: This title's source doesn't get new chapters.
toast.paused: Paused — automatic updates are off for this title.
